If demand rises, we will expand our capacity.
If a competitor enters, we will compete on quality.
If a downturn hits, costs will be cut.
Unless the market collapses, we will keep investing.
Last year, demand rose and we expanded the plant.
Izabel, I have read the market outlook. It looks strong but uncertain. What is our plan if things change?
Good question, Andy. If demand keeps rising, we will expand our capacity and break into North America faster.
And if a bigger competitor enters the segment?
If a big player moves in, we will not compete on price. We will compete on quality and lead time, where we are stronger.
That makes sense. The board worries about a downturn. What happens then?
If a downturn hits, we have a contingency: costs will be cut, and the launch will be postponed, not cancelled. Unless the whole market collapses, we will keep investing in research.
Good. And how do you plan to reduce the overall risk?
We will diversify. As long as the core sensor line stays strong, we will add two new product families and grow our market share step by step.
If demand rises, we will expand our capacity and break into North America.
If a downturn hits, costs will be cut and the launch postponed.
As long as the core line stays strong, we will diversify.

Hi Izabel, this is Paul, your market analyst, with the outlook before Thursday. The headline is simple: the market is strong but volatile. Here is how I read it. If demand keeps rising the way it has this quarter, you will need more capacity by the summer, so plan for it now. If a large competitor enters your segment, and I think one will, do not compete on price. As long as your quality and your lead time stay ahead, you will keep your margin and your market share. One warning: if a downturn hits, it will hit fast, so keep a contingency ready. But unless the whole sector collapses, the trend is up. If you diversify now, you will be in a strong position next year. Talk on Thursday.

Good morning, Izabel. It is Rachel, from the board, with a few thoughts before the call. I have read Paul's outlook, and I agree with most of it. The board's view is this: if the new sensor line performs, we will back a full launch and support you breaking into North America. If it underperforms, we will not panic; we will slow down and protect the margin. On competition, we trust your instinct. If a bigger player moves in, we will compete on quality, exactly as you said. And please keep diversifying. As long as the core business is healthy, every new product family reduces our risk. One last thing: if you ever need more capital for capacity, ask early. We would rather plan than react. See you Thursday.
